skill.name: cost-optimization
skill.description: Optimize cloud costs through resource rightsizing, tagging strategies, reserved instances, and spending analysis. Use when reducing cloud expenses, analyzing infrastructure costs, or implementing cost governance policies.
skill.summary: Guide cloud teams through rightsizing, tagging, reserved capacity, budget alerts, and storage lifecycle planning.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, C2 keywords, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown fences, cloud service names, Terraform examples, and cost guidance. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable scripts, network calls, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences around pricing notes and Terraform examples. They do not execute commands or instruct the assistant to run shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters are visible Markdown delimiters and the surrounding text is documentation. No command interpreter, subprocess call, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cloud Service Terms Flagged as C2 Keywords
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword hits correspond to EC2 cost optimization text and an AWS/EC2 CloudWatch namespace. These are normal cloud platform references, not command and control indicators.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is AWS pricing and Terraform monitoring configuration. No network endpoint, beaconing behavior, or malware control channel is described.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hit appears on the frontmatter description for cloud cost optimization. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing routine, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is natural-language metadata about cost analysis and governance. I did not find evidence of weak cryptographic code or instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cloud Configuration Terms Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance hits occur in Terraform bucket configuration and Azure Hybrid Benefit guidance. They do not enumerate local systems, collect host data, or request sensitive environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are ordinary infrastructure documentation. There is no instruction to inspect the host, enumerate files, or gather credentials.

content.value_statement: Cloud spending can grow through oversized resources, weak tagging, and idle services. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured FinOps guidance for AWS, Azure, and GCP.
actual_capabilities: Explains AWS reserved instances, Savings Plans, Spot Instances, and S3 lifecycle cost controls.
Summarizes Azure reserved VM instances, Azure Hybrid Benefit, and Advisor recommendations.
Covers GCP committed use discounts, sustained use discounts, and preemptible VM use cases.
Provides Terraform examples for tagging, budget alerts, S3 lifecycle rules, and autoscaling policies.
Lists a practical checklist for rightsizing, idle resource cleanup, budget alerts, and ongoing reviews.
limitations: Does not connect to cloud billing APIs or read live account costs.
Does not calculate exact savings from a real invoice without user-provided data.
Does not replace finance approval, procurement review, or cloud provider pricing validation.
Example Terraform must be adapted and tested before use in production.

use_cases.title: Reduce Monthly Cloud Waste
use_cases.description: Identify common savings actions such as rightsizing, deleting unused resources, and applying storage lifecycle policies.

use_cases.title: Create FinOps Governance
use_cases.description: Define tagging, budget alerts, cost dashboards, and weekly review practices for accountable cloud spending.

use_cases.title: Plan Reserved Capacity
use_cases.description: Compare reserved instances, savings plans, committed use discounts, and spot options for steady or flexible workloads.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Savings
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of obvious cost reduction actions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my AWS, Azure, or GCP environment description and list the top cost optimization actions I should consider first.

prompt_templates.title: Design Tagging Governance
prompt_templates.scenario: Create consistent ownership and cost allocation practices.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cloud cost tagging strategy for environments, projects, owners, and cost centers. Include enforcement and reporting guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Pricing Models
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose discounts for predictable and variable workloads.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare reserved capacity, savings plans, committed use discounts, and spot or preemptible instances for these workloads and constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Build a FinOps Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-day FinOps roadmap for a multi-cloud organization. Include rightsizing, storage lifecycle, budget alerts, anomaly detection, and governance milestones.

output_examples.input: We run production web services on AWS with growing EC2, RDS, and S3 costs.
output_examples.output: A prioritized savings plan covering EC2 rightsizing, Savings Plans evaluation, RDS environment sizing, S3 lifecycle transitions, and monthly budget alerts.

output_examples.input: Our teams do not tag cloud resources consistently across projects.
output_examples.output: A tagging model with Environment, Project, CostCenter, Owner, and ManagedBy fields, plus enforcement and reporting recommendations.

output_examples.input: We need guidance for batch jobs that can tolerate interruption.
output_examples.output: A workload recommendation that considers Spot Instances or preemptible VMs, interruption handling, fallback capacity, and monitoring requirements.

best_practices: Start with cost visibility, tagging, and budget alerts before making major architecture changes.
Validate reserved capacity only after confirming stable usage patterns and business commitments.
Review optimization actions regularly because cloud pricing, workload demand, and product options change.
anti_patterns: Do not buy long-term commitments before measuring steady utilization.
Do not delete or downsize production resources without performance and reliability review.
Do not rely on cost reductions that require teams to ignore security, compliance, or backup needs.

faq.question: Does this skill read my cloud bill automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples based on information you provide.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers AWS, Azure, and GCP, with references to multi-cloud cost tools.

faq.question: Can it create Terraform for cost controls?
faq.answer: It includes Terraform examples for tagging, S3 lifecycle rules, budget alerts, and autoscaling.

faq.question: Is it useful for FinOps teams?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports tagging strategy, budget alerts, cost dashboards, and review workflows.

faq.question: Can it guarantee a savings percentage?
faq.answer: No. Actual savings depend on usage, contracts, pricing, regions, and workload requirements.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found false-positive static findings and no malicious behavior.
